tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, significant proteinuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, and ketonuria, with low urine specific gravity and a low HbA1c. These findings suggest impaired renal function, likely nephrotic-range proteinuria, and possible tubular dysfunction. The presence of glucosuria with normal blood glucose and low HbA1c may indicate renal glycosuria. Urine abnormalities (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) further support renal pathology. Recommend nephrology evaluation.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
